frozen_components: ConvNeXt-Tiny classifier (frozen after training for all SAE analysis and ablations)
architecture.summary: A ConvNeXt-Tiny classifier fine-tuned on FGVC-Aircraft extracts spatial activations from its final feature stage (before global average pooling). These patch-level activation vectors are fed into a sparse autoencoder trained with ReLU encoder, linear decoder, reconstruction loss, and L1 sparsity penalty. The SAE learns an overcomplete sparse latent representation. Interpretation proceeds via top-activating patch inspection, then two ablation strategies: input-space (Gaussian blur of image regions identified by high SAE feature activation) and feature-space (zeroing individual SAE latent features in the internal representation, then continuing the forward pass).

biblio.title: Analyzing Visual Aircraft Representations with Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: The ConvNeXt-Tiny classifier achieves an accuracy of [value not stated] and a macro F1 score of [value not stated] on the test set
The sparse autoencoder achieves an explained variance score of 0.9978 on the test split activations

Several sparse features correspond to recognizable aircraft structures and visual patterns, including landing gear, flap track fairings, fuselage windows, cockpit region, engine & wing adjacent regions, and biplane struts

claims.dataset: FGVC-Aircraft (top-50 test patches for eight selected SAE features)
claims.statement: Expanded target-region blurring produces a true-class logit drop of 4.503 compared with 1.074 for expanded random-region blurring, averaged over eight selected SAE features

claims.dataset: FGVC-Aircraft (top-50 test patches for eight selected SAE features)
claims.statement: Expanded target-region blurring produces a classification margin drop of 4.909 compared with 1.106 for expanded random-region blurring, averaged over eight selected SAE features

claims.dataset: FGVC-Aircraft (top-50 test patches for eight selected SAE features)
claims.statement: Expanded target-region blurring produces prediction flips in 0.220 (22%) of cases, compared with 0.045 (4.5%) for expanded random-region blurring, averaged over eight selected SAE features

claims.dataset: FGVC-Aircraft (top-50 test patches for eight selected SAE features)
claims.metric: true-class logit drop; classification margin drop; prediction flip rate
claims.statement: Global feature-space suppression produces a true-class logit drop of 0.098 and a classification margin drop of 0.113, averaged over eight selected SAE features, with no prediction flips occurring

SAE features are not perfectly monosemantic; they exhibit polysemanticity, activating on multiple unrelated patterns, and do not cleanly align with isolated human-defined aircraft parts. Feature-space ablation produces much smaller effects than input-space ablation, and individual sparse features are not standalone causal units in the classifier representation.
